page: Microsoft Azure D16ads_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the Dadv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 8 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz and is equipped with 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Storage is bundled with a 945 GB local disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. No GPUs are included.

Performance benchmarks reveal a strong single-core profile. The instance achieves top-tier status in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench text processing, both ranking in the top 10%. Geekbench single-core performance is also strong, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core and memory benchmarks generally align with average tiers. A notable performance tradeoff is observed in memory bandwidth; while cached small-block reads and large-block writes perform at average levels, uncached large-block read memory bandwidth is weak, falling into the bottom 25% at 37.84 GB/sec. Large-scale LLM prompt processing is also a bottleneck, with the 70B model processing speed ranking in the bottom 10%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D16ads_v6 offers a balanced resource density suitable for mainstream enterprise applications. The inclusion of a 945 GB local SSD provides high-speed temporary storage without requiring external disk attachments, optimizing the cost-to-resource ratio for data-intensive operations. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, text processing pipelines, web serving, database operations, and small-model LLM text generation where single-threaded execution speed is prioritized over massive memory bandwidth.
description: Microsoft Azure D16ads_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine in the Dadv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it includes a 945 GB local SSD and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks a GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance, alongside strong single-core Geekbench scores. However, multi-core execution and memory write speeds remain average, and uncached large-block memory read bandwidth is weak, ranking in the bottom 25%. This resource profile delivers balanced cost efficiency for mainstream enterprise workloads. The D16ads_v6 is suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, database operations, and text processing tasks that benefit from its strong single-threaded performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16ads_v6 is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 945 GB local SSD, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance alongside average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16ads_v6 is a general-purpose instance with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 945 GB local SSD, optimized for single-core workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, a local SSD, and strong single-core performance for mainstream enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor at 3.7 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory paired with a 945 GB local SSD
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Tradeoff of weak uncached large-block memory read bandwidth
Suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and database operations
